Genes.Breeding_or_Biofortification_Value: Potential for breeding disease-resistant and lodging-resistant rice varieties through enhanced lignin content; marker for lignin pathway engineering
Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect_on_Nutrient: Overexpression of OsMYB110 significantly increases lignin content (~48%) in rice leaves by transcriptionally activating multiple lignin biosynthetic genes (OsPAL1, OsPAL2, OsPAL3, Os4CL3, Os4CL5)
Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression transgenic lines (OsMYB110-OX); dual luciferase reporter assay (activates OsPAL1/2/3 promoters); yeast one-hybrid assay (binds OsPAL1/2/3, Os4CL3, Os4CL5 promoters); qRT-PCR; lignin content measurement (acetyl bromide method)
Genes.Enzyme_Name_or_Function: R2R3-type MYB transcription factor; activates lignin biosynthetic pathway genes (PAL, 4CL)
Genes.Experimental_Materials: OsMYB110-OX transgenic lines in ZH11 background (driven by maize ubiquitin promoter)
Genes.Expression_Pattern: High expression in plumules, radicles, roots, and mature leaves; co-expressed with lignin biosynthetic genes across multiple tissues
Genes.Flux_or_Direction_Change: Increases flux toward lignin biosynthesis by activating upstream phenylpropanoid genes (PAL1/2/3) and downstream 4CL3/5
Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Co-expression network analysis (RMRN); guilt-by-association using known regulator OsMYB30 as bait
Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: OsMYB110 is phylogenetically related to known lignin regulator OsMYB30 and shares co-regulated genes with OsMYB30. The gene was identified from RMRN co-expression cluster XII (multiple tissues).
Genes.Nutrient_Assay_or_Profiling_Method: Acetyl bromide (AcBr) method for lignin content
Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome (CREP database, GEO: GSE19024); metabolome (OMIX510); co-expression network analysis
Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Enhanced disease resistance: OsMYB110-OX lines showed significantly smaller lesions after Xoo (PXO99 and 341) infection compared to ZH11
Genes.Quantitative_Nutrient_Change: ~48% increase in lignin content in OsMYB110-OX leaves compared to ZH11
Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Transcriptional activation; directly binds promoters of OsPAL1, OsPAL2, OsPAL3, Os4CL3, Os4CL5 to activate their expression
Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus (confirmed by YFP fusion in rice protoplasts, co-localized with OsGhd7-CFP nuclear marker and DAPI staining)
Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsMYB110 is an R2R3-MYB transcription factor that positively regulates lignin biosynthesis in rice. 2. Overexpression of OsMYB110 increases lignin content by ~48% in leaves at the two-tiller stage. 3. OsMYB110 directly binds and activates the promoters of OsPAL1, OsPAL2, OsPAL3, Os4CL3, and Os4CL5 (confirmed by Y1H and dual LUC assays). 4. OsMYB110-OX transgenic lines show enhanced resistance to bacterial blight pathogen Xoo. 5. OsMYB110 was identified through co-expression network analysis using known regulator OsMYB30 as bait in the RMRN dataset.
Genes.Units_and_Basis: relative lignin content, leaf tissue at two-tiller stage
Genes.Variety: ZH11 (transgenic background); Zhenshan 97 and Minghui 63 (expression profiling)

Genes.Breeding_or_Biofortification_Value: Potential for breeding disease-resistant and lodging-resistant rice varieties; bidirectional control (OX/RNAi) confirms utility as breeding target for lignin content modulation
Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect_on_Nutrient: Overexpression of OsMYB55 increases lignin content (~15%), while RNAi knockdown decreases lignin content (~13%) in rice leaves, demonstrating bidirectional control of lignin accumulation
Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression (OsMYB55-OX) and RNAi (OsMYB55-RNAi) transgenic lines; qRT-PCR of downstream lignin genes; lignin content measurement (acetyl bromide method)
Genes.Enzyme_Name_or_Function: R2R3-type MYB transcription factor; regulates lignin biosynthetic pathway genes (4CL, PAL6, C4H)
Genes.Experimental_Materials: OsMYB55-OX and OsMYB55-RNAi transgenic lines in ZH11 background
Genes.Expression_Pattern: High expression in plumules, radicles, roots, and mature leaves; co-expressed with lignin biosynthetic genes
Genes.Flux_or_Direction_Change: Increases flux toward lignin biosynthesis by activating Os4CL3, Os4CL5, OsPAL6, and OsC4H
Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Co-expression network analysis (RMRN); guilt-by-association using known regulator OsMYB30 as bait
Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: OsMYB55 is phylogenetically related to OsMYB30 (known lignin regulator). Both gain-of-function and loss-of-function evidence support its role. Note: the accession LOC_Os01g07120 appears to be shared with OsDREB2A in the Methods section, which may be a typographical error in the paper.
Genes.Nutrient_Assay_or_Profiling_Method: Acetyl bromide (AcBr) method for lignin content
Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Enhanced disease resistance in OX lines (smaller Xoo lesions); reduced disease resistance in RNAi lines (larger Xoo lesions) compared to ZH11
Genes.Quantitative_Nutrient_Change: ~15% increase in lignin in OsMYB55-OX leaves; ~13% decrease in lignin in OsMYB55-RNAi leaves compared to ZH11
Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Transcriptional activation of lignin biosynthetic genes
Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsMYB55 is an R2R3-MYB transcription factor that positively regulates lignin biosynthesis. 2. Overexpression increases lignin content by ~15%, while RNAi knockdown decreases it by ~13% in leaves. 3. OsMYB55-OX lines show upregulation of Os4CL3, Os4CL5, OsPAL6, and OsC4H; RNAi lines show their downregulation. 4. OsMYB55-OX lines have enhanced resistance to Xoo; RNAi lines show reduced resistance. 5. Identified through RMRN co-expression network using OsMYB30 as bait.
Genes.Units_and_Basis: relative lignin content, leaf tissue at two-tiller stage
Genes.Variety: ZH11 (transgenic background); Zhenshan 97 and Minghui 63 (expression profiling)

Genes.Breeding_or_Biofortification_Value: Novel regulator of seed glycerophospholipid composition; potential target for improving rice seed nutritional quality (phospholipid profile) and abiotic stress tolerance; dual benefit of quality improvement and stress resistance
Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect_on_Nutrient: Overexpression of OsDREB2A significantly increases monoacylglycerophospholipid (LPC, LPE) content and decreases diacylglycerophospholipid (PC, PE, PS) content in rice seeds, likely through activation of phospholipase A family genes
Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression transgenic lines (OsDREB2A-OX); dual luciferase reporter assay (activates OsPLAIVa, OsPLAIIIa, OsPLDα promoters); qRT-PCR of downstream glycerophospholipid metabolism genes; targeted metabolic profiling of seed phospholipids
Genes.Enzyme_Name_or_Function: AP2/EREBP domain transcription factor (DREB subfamily); activates glycerophospholipid metabolism genes
Genes.Experimental_Materials: OsDREB2A-OX transgenic lines in ZH11 background (driven by maize ubiquitin promoter)
Genes.Expression_Pattern: Seed-specific expression; co-expressed with glycerophospholipid metabolism genes and glycerophospholipid metabolites in cluster VIII (seed cluster)
Genes.Flux_or_Direction_Change: Increases glycerophospholipid degradation flux: promotes conversion of diacylglycerophospholipids (PC, PE, PS) to monoacylglycerophospholipids (LPC, LPE) by activating phospholipase genes (OsPLAIVa, OsPLAIIIa, OsPLDα)
Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Unbiased co-expression network screening based on tissue specificity (seed-specific cluster VIII in RMRN)
Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: PEG6000 (15%, 20%) dehydration stress; NaCl (100, 200 mM) salt stress
Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: OsDREB2A is homologous to AtDREB2A. This is the first report linking DREB subfamily TFs to glycerophospholipid metabolism. The co-expression coefficient between OsDREB2A and OsPLAIVa is 0.71. OsPLAIVa is proposed as the key downstream effector mediating the phospholipid remodeling effect. The accession LOC_Os01g07120 is listed for both OsMYB55 and OsDREB2A in the Methods, suggesting a possible typo in the paper for one of these genes.
Genes.Nutrient_Assay_or_Profiling_Method: UPLC-MS/MS (targeted and nontargeted metabolic profiling); LC-ESI-QQQ-MS/MS
Genes.Nutrient_Trait_Metric: content; composition ratio (monoacyl vs diacyl glycerophospholipids)
Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome (CREP database, GEO: GSE19024); metabolome (OMIX510); co-expression network
Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Enhanced abiotic stress tolerance: OsDREB2A-OX seedlings showed higher survival rate under PEG6000 (dehydration) and NaCl (salt) stress; better shoot and root growth under stress
Genes.Quantitative_Nutrient_Change: Most monoacylglycerophospholipids (LPC, LPE) significantly increased; diacylglycerophospholipids (PC, PE, PS) decreased in OsDREB2A-OX seeds compared to ZH11; OsPLAIVa expression increased ~2-fold (P=3.5E-4)
Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Transcriptional activation; recognizes DRE elements with A/GCCGAC core motif on promoters of glycerophospholipid metabolism genes; directly activates OsPLAIVa, OsPLAIIIa, and OsPLDα promoters
Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsDREB2A is an AP2/EREBP transcription factor identified as a novel regulator of glycerophospholipid metabolism in rice seeds. 2. OsDREB2A-OX lines show significantly increased monoacylglycerophospholipids (LPC, LPE) and decreased diacylglycerophospholipids (PC, PE, PS) in seeds. 3. OsDREB2A directly activates the promoters of OsPLAIVa, OsPLAIIIa, and OsPLDα (confirmed by dual LUC assay). 4. OsPLAIVa expression is stably increased ~2-fold in OX lines, suggesting it is the primary downstream effector. 5. OsDREB2A-OX also confers enhanced dehydration and salt stress tolerance. 6. Identified through unbiased tissue-specificity-based screening in RMRN cluster VIII (seed-specific).
Genes.Variety: ZH11 (transgenic background); Zhenshan 97 and Minghui 63 (expression profiling)

Genes.Breeding_or_Biofortification_Value: Key enzyme target for modifying seed phospholipid composition; could be used in combination with OsDREB2A for seed quality improvement
Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect_on_Nutrient: OsPLAIVa is the key downstream effector of OsDREB2A; its upregulation (~2-fold) in OsDREB2A-OX lines is associated with increased monoacylglycerophospholipids and decreased diacylglycerophospholipids in seeds
Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR in OsDREB2A-OX transgenic lines showing ~2-fold upregulation; dual luciferase reporter assay confirming OsDREB2A activates OsPLAIVa promoter; co-expression analysis (r=0.71 with OsDREB2A)
Genes.Enzyme_Name_or_Function: Phospholipase A (PLA family); involved in glycerophospholipid degradation, catalyzing hydrolysis of diacylglycerophospholipids to monoacylglycerophospholipids
Genes.Experimental_Materials: OsDREB2A-OX transgenic lines in ZH11 background (indirect validation via TF overexpression)
Genes.Expression_Pattern: Seed-specific expression; co-expressed with OsDREB2A (r=0.71) and glycerophospholipid metabolites
Genes.Flux_or_Direction_Change: Promotes degradation of diacylglycerophospholipids to monoacylglycerophospholipids in seeds
Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: OsPLAIVa is highly homologous to OsPLAIIIa and OsMHZ11, all belonging to the PLA family involved in glycerophospholipid degradation. It is proposed as the primary effector through which OsDREB2A controls glycerophospholipid metabolism.
Genes.Quantitative_Nutrient_Change: Expression increased ~2-fold (P=3.5E-4) in OsDREB2A-OX lines; correlated with increased LPC/LPE in seeds
Genes.Reaction_Product_or_Immediate_Output: Monoacylglycerophospholipids (LPC, LPE) and free fatty acids
Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Transcriptionally activated by OsDREB2A via DRE elements in promoter
Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsPLAIVa is a phospholipase A family enzyme involved in glycerophospholipid degradation in rice seeds. 2. Its promoter is directly activated by OsDREB2A (confirmed by dual LUC assay). 3. Expression is stably and significantly increased ~2-fold in OsDREB2A-OX lines. 4. Upregulation of OsPLAIVa correlates with increased monoacylglycerophospholipids (LPC, LPE) and decreased diacylglycerophospholipids in seeds. 5. Co-expression coefficient with OsDREB2A is 0.71.
Genes.Units_and_Basis: relative expression and metabolite content in seeds

Title: Rice metabolic regulatory network spanning the entire life cycle
